summary: Remote code execution dropper: index.js fetches and eval()s a GitHub Gist on require()
details: The package's main entry (index.js) executes top-level code on every require()/import that fetches a GitHub Gist owned by user `metabrainai000` (gist id 198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90) via the GitHub API and passes the response body directly to eval(). Specifically, index.js:2-10 builds `https://api.github.com/gists/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90`, reads `gistData.files[firstFileName].content`, and calls `eval(scriptText)` in the installer's Node.js process. The Gist content is mutable and controlled by an unrelated third party, so any consumer that loads this package runs whatever JavaScript the Gist owner chooses at that moment. The package is published under the @sqlite-node scope and advertises itself as 'A SQLite Toolkit for node', but contains no SQLite code — only the dropper — strongly suggesting impersonation of SQLite-related tooling to lure developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
